Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Grants

Grants Status Requests To submit a request, visit NASA General Information Request Form and complete the form. You will receive an automated email with the most commonly requested grant status information. Important Instructions: How to Fill Out the Form: Memorandum for NASA Grantee Community...

NASA's streamlined grant status system enables grantees to self-serve common requests through automated responses, reducing administrative burden on agency personnel.

See NASA →

Military Apr 6, 2026

Lady of Larissa Flies Again: Incredible New Footage Emerges of the Secretive RQ-180 Spy Drone

New videos give an even clearer view – unquestionably our best ever – of the legendary classified stealth drone, previously seen making an emergency landing in Greece last month. Last month, we reported on images taken in Larissa, Greece, showing what we, and others, concluded – based on prior...

Newly emerged footage of the RQ-180 stealth drone provides unprecedented visual confirmation of this long-classified aircraft's design, potentially affecting intelligence community operational security protocols and aircraft recognition capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The Near Side of the Moon

The astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft captured this photo of the Moon’s near side on April 4, 2026. The image features dark patches at center and right; these are ancient lava flows, which are unique to this side of the Moon. The Artemis II astronauts – astronauts Victor Glover, Reid...

Artemis II's lunar imagery documents the geological features that will guide future landing site selection and resource assessment for sustained human Moon exploration.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Amazon Leo to serve as launchpad for more Delta brand experiences

The proverbial ‘gravitational pull’ of low-latency Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-powered inflight connectivity is continuing unabated in commercial aviation, as Delta Air Lines earmarks Amazon Leo for an initial 500 tails from 2028. Announced on 31 March, the arrangement springboards off the US...

Delta's commitment to Amazon's LEO constellation positions satellite connectivity as the connectivity standard for major carriers, potentially triggering industry-wide infrastructure transitions by 2028.

See Runway Girl Network →

Safety Apr 6, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern aviation SMS shifts the industry from investigating accidents after they occur to identifying and mitigating hazards before they endanger flights.

Military Apr 6, 2026

USAF F-16s Flying With AGM-158 JASSMs in Operation Epic Fury Against Iran

Long-range JASSM strikes still continue on Iranian targets, particularly against missile and drone production facilities. U.S. Air Force F-16s currently deployed in support of Operation Epic Fury have been shown in official photos carrying the AGM-158 Joint-Air-to-Surface Standoff Missile (JASSM)...

JASSM deployment demonstrates the F-16's extended standoff capability, allowing fighters to strike Iranian production facilities from safer distances while reducing aircraft exposure to air defense systems.

See The Aviationist →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

National STOL Announces 2026 Competition Schedule

Series plans eight events across the U.S. beginning in April.

STOL competitions are expanding to eight regional events, signaling growing industry interest in short-takeoff-landing aircraft capabilities for underserved markets.

A Look At The Salaries Of The US Big 3 Airline Pilots In 2026

Airlines are in a fierce competition for top talent, and pilots are reaping the rewards.

Pilot compensation escalation signals airlines' recognition that crew retention directly impacts operational reliability and fleet availability in a tight labor market.

Drones Apr 6, 2026

Invisible drone defense helped secure the massive JUNO Awards crowd

When tens of thousands of fans gather under one roof, security doesn’t just stop at the doors; it extends to the skies above. That was exactly the case at Canada’s biggest night in music, the JUNO Awards, where a quiet but critical layer of anti-drone protection was keeping watch overhead. more…...

High-profile venues are increasingly deploying counter-drone technology as standard security infrastructure alongside traditional perimeter controls.

Organizations with psychologically safe environments see 3x higher incident reporting rates, enabling proactive hazard identification before accidents occur.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

British Airways Axes Airbus A380 Flights On This Major US Route: Here's Why

The carrier has made an unexpected decision, removing all A380 flights from London Heathrow to this very popular destination for the upcoming winter.

BA's A380 withdrawal reflects broader airline profitability pressures as fuel costs and maintenance expenses outweigh premium cabin revenue on even flagship routes.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.

Early in my career at SpaceX, I was the only person dedicated to training the mission control team for Dragon. For three years, I built the training for the operations team. The documentation came second, always the thing I’d get to later, always the thing that felt like overhead against the actual...

Lunar base operators must establish comprehensive software training and documentation protocols now to prevent the operational bottlenecks that plagued early commercial spaceflight programs.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Press Release: Newly rebranded Aeroplay to unveil Nucleus OS

Aeroplay Entertainment, a leading Content Service Provider (CSP) in the aviation industry, is entering an exciting new chapter. The company is rebranding under is Passenger Experience Partner vision to deliver more integrated and passenger-centric inflight solutions. This transformation is...

Aeroplay's shift toward integrated passenger experience platforms signals consolidation in the IFE market toward holistic solutions rather than point products.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Airbus Is Considering Final Assembly In India For ATR Turboprops

European planemaker Airbus does indeed have most of its operations in Europe. However, the firm also has commercial aircraft assembly lines in Canada, the United States, and China. The manufacturing giant’s operations also include ATR - a 50/50 joint venture with Italy’s Leonardo. And so when it...

Airbus's potential India manufacturing hub would extend ATR's regional aircraft production beyond Europe, tapping into Asia's growing demand for turboprops while leveraging lower labor costs.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 6, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit confusion at LAX's Runway 24R demonstrates how even at major hubs, ambiguous taxiway routing during high-traffic periods creates recurring incursion risks requiring procedural clarification.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Corporate Apr 6, 2026

Fired Or Retired? Air Canada's CEO Announces Departure From Airline

Was Michael Rousseau, the CEO (well, soon-to-be-former CEO) of Air Canada just fired? Well, not officially, but it certainly looks like he was forced out. It was on March 30th that the airline’s Board of Directors announced that Rousseau would be retiring by the end of the third quarter of 2026,...

Rousseau's forced departure reflects Air Canada's board dissatisfaction with leadership amid operational or financial pressures, signaling potential strategic shifts in the airline's direction.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha

St. Petersburg, FL — April 6, 2026 — Seagate Space Corporation announced today a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Firefly Aerospace to collaborate on the development of an offshore launch platform […]...

Offshore launch infrastructure partnerships signal emerging demand for alternative spaceport capacity to address ground-based facility congestion.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Military space programs confront hidden supply constraints

Industry report warns components – from optical links to valves – may become chokepoints...

Military aerospace supply chains face critical vulnerabilities in specialized components like optical systems and valves that lack alternative suppliers, threatening program timelines and national security.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Artemis 2 ready to fly around the moon

The Artemis 2 mission will swing around the moon April 6, setting a distance record as astronauts study part of the lunar farside.

Artemis 2's farside lunar trajectory will generate critical thermal and radiation data essential for sustaining future crewed deep-space missions beyond low Earth orbit.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Apr 6, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal helicopter cause because pilots systematically underestimate closure speeds and overestimate their reaction time in low-altitude operations.

New Whitepaper Outlines Strategic Role of HAPS in Enabling 6G Use Cases

RA’ANANA, Israel and HAMILTON, Ontario April 6th, 2026 — D-Fend Solutions, the leader in field-proven...

RF-based counter-drone technology is gaining institutional recognition, suggesting industry-wide validation of electromagnetic neutralization over kinetic methods for airspace security.

Drones Apr 6, 2026

American Civil Liberties Union – Drones for them but not for us

The drone sightings all over Europe are frustrating me somewhat, but a new report from...

ACLU's drone policy critique reveals inconsistency in advocacy positions regarding surveillance and operational autonomy standards.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Qatar Airways Grounds All Airbus A380s For 2 Months: Here's What's Next

Qatar Airways has made a bold move, grounding all eight of its Airbus A380s in April and May. All its aircraft are now back in Doha.

Scheduled A380 groundings signal planned maintenance or fleet reconfiguration rather than safety issues, allowing Qatar Airways to redistribute capacity across smaller aircraft routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers—airline, airport, and flight—offers more immediate emissions and capacity gains than waiting for aircraft technology transitions alone.

See MDPI →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

5 Reasons Why Airlines Are Upgrading Cabin Interiors Instead Of Replacing Aircraft

Carriers have many reasons for choosing this pathway forward.

Interior upgrades cost 5-10% of new aircraft prices while improving passenger experience and airline revenue per seat without aircraft replacement cycles.

Lufthansa is marking its 100th anniversary with two special simultaneous celebratory flights on April 6, 2026. At 15.00…...

Airlines competing on cabin comfort rather than pricing may capture premium leisure travelers willing to pay for economy-plus experiences without upgrading to business class.

Drones Apr 6, 2026

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2 series, Matrice 600

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's end-of-support timeline for legacy professional drones signals operators must migrate to newer platforms or risk losing critical firmware updates and technical support.

See DroneDJ →

Military Apr 6, 2026

5 Aircraft Used By The US Military That Are Larger Than Commercial Jets

The largest USAF aircraft are converted Boeing commercial aircraft, although it also operates massive purpose-built bomber and transport aircraft.

Military cargo and command variants of commercial aircraft often exceed standard airliner dimensions, expanding payload and operational range beyond typical commercial constraints.

Military Apr 6, 2026

France plans to boost drone production by 400%: media reports

France is preparing to significantly expand its stocks of bombs, missiles and drones, according to digital news outlet…...

France's massive drone production expansion signals European nations are rapidly militarizing unmanned systems capabilities amid geopolitical tensions.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

One Stop To The US: Air New Zealand Set To Become Western Sydney’s 1st International Airline

The long-awaited Western Sydney International Airport is set to lure in airlines with its lack of curfew.

The absence of night-flight restrictions at Western Sydney gives Air New Zealand a 24-hour operational window unavailable at Sydney's congested primary airport, enabling more efficient trans-Pacific scheduling.

Leadership visibility in premium cabins signals confidence in premium product quality during periods of operational scrutiny or transition.

Italy warns of jet fuel shortages at four airports, others may follow suit

Several airports in Europe may soon start facing fuel shortages, warned jet fuel distributors. Air bp Italia, the…...

European fuel distribution infrastructure vulnerabilities could cascade across multiple hubs, forcing airlines to implement costly rerouting and contingency fueling strategies.

Aerospace Apr 5, 2026

Isaacman defends NASA budget proposal despite steep cuts

NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman defended a fiscal year 2027 budget proposal that would cut the agency’s budget by nearly 25%.

NASA's proposed 25% budget reduction threatens long-term space infrastructure investments critical to commercial aerospace partnerships and launch cadence.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 5, 2026

NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2

NASA has stopped work on a second mobile launch platform intended for an upgraded version of the Space Launch System the agency no longer plans to develop.

NASA's termination of the ML2 program reflects shifting priorities toward commercial launch providers, potentially reducing long-term costs but raising questions about SLS's future competitiveness.

See SpaceNews →
